mission.title: Install and Configure Valkey
mission.description: Valkey is a high-performance distributed key-value database optimized for caching and real-time workloads. It is suitable for applications requiring fast data access and supports a wide range of data structures. Key trade-offs include the need for careful configuration and resource management to achieve optimal performance.

steps.title: Add the Valkey Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Valkey Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Valkey using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add valkey https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey/releases/download/v9.0.3

```

> This command adds the Valkey repository for version 9.0.3.

steps.title: Install Valkey CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Valkey to function correctly within your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey/releases/download/v9.0.3/valkey-crds.yaml

```

> Ensure you have the correct CRDs for version 9.0.3.

steps.title: Install Valkey using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Valkey in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install valkey valkey/valkey --namespace valkey --create-namespace --version 9.0.3

```

> This installs Valkey in the 'valkey' namespace.

steps.title: Create a Valkey Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an instance of the Valkey Custom Resource to deploy the database.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: valkey.io/v1
kind: Valkey
metadata:
  name: my-valkey
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> This creates a Valkey instance named 'my-valkey' with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: valkey.io/v1
kind: Valkey
metadata:
  name: my-valkey
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify Valkey is running
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Valkey pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n valkey

```

> You should see the Valkey pods in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Valkey
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Valkey installation and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in Valkey.

```bash

helm uninstall valkey --namespace valkey

kubectl delete namespace valkey

```

> This command uninstalls Valkey from the specified namespace.
> This command deletes the namespace and all orphaned resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Valkey
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade Valkey to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp my-valkey:/data /tmp/valkey-backup

helm upgrade valkey valkey/valkey --namespace valkey --version 9.0.3

kubectl get pods -n valkey

```

> Backup your data from the Valkey pod.
> This command upgrades Valkey to version 9.0.3.
> Verify that the Valkey pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Valkey'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Valkey service is not running or the network policy is blocking access.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Valkey pods and ensure the service is correctly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources available to schedule the Valkey pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits on your nodes or reduce the resource requests for Valkey.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Valkey pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually happens if there is a misconfiguration in the Valkey deployment or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Custom Resource not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Valkey Custom Resource was not created or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Custom Resource definition is applied correctly and the resource is created.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new caching algorithms and improved data replication features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed support for legacy data structures.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from version 8.x, ensure to migrate your data using the provided migration tools.

resolution.summary: You should see the Valkey pods in a Running state when executing 'kubectl get pods -n valkey'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
